summary: Ukrainian forces used dozens of Shahed drones in a large‑scale launch targeting multiple southern locations, while Russian attacks on the Starobelsk college resulted in 70 casualties, including 21 deaths. UAV activity was reported across northern oblasts, with several strike‑capable drones operating near Chernihiv, Sumy, and Kirovohrad regions. Ukraine received a new IRIS‑T air‑defence launcher and struck a Russian oil‑refining plant in Saratov.
key_points: Ukrainian forces launched 97‑194 Shahed drones in an hour against Primorsko‑Ahtarsk, Shatalove, Millerovo, Hvardiyiske, Orel, and Kursk (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67333).
Russian attack on the Starobelsk college involved 16 UAVs, causing 70 injuries, 21 deaths, and extensive damage (Source: @tass_agency/378497).
Multiple UAVs were observed over northern oblasts: Chernihiv, Sumy, and Kirovohrad, including strike‑capable models moving toward Kyiv and Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/63514, @kpszsu/63507, @kpszsu/63522).
Ukraine received an IRIS‑T surface‑to‑air missile system launcher (Source: @UkraineNow/81583).
Ukrainian forces attacked an oil‑refining plant in Saratov, Russia (Source: @UkraineNow/81583).
Air‑raid alerts were issued over Kyiv at 20:14 and lifted at 20:39 on 01‑06‑2026 (Source: @eRadarrua/94636, @eRadarrua/94660).
Russian forces dropped three FAB‑250 aerial bombs on Druzhkivka, injuring five civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/81596).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces launched a massive Shahed drone barrage targeting several southern settlements, while Russian UAV attacks on the Starobelsk college caused 70 casualties, including 21 fatalities. UAV activity intensified across northern oblasts, with strike‑capable drones operating near Chernihiv, Sumy, and Kirovohrad. Ukraine bolstered its air defence with an IRIS‑T system and struck a Russian oil‑refining plant in Saratov.

points: Ukraine deployed a large Shahed drone swarm, estimated at 97‑194 units within an hour, against six southern locations including Primorsko‑Ahtarsk and Shatalove, indicating a coordinated offensive (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67333).
Ukrainian forces reported strike‑capable UAVs operating from the north of Chernihiv and Sumy oblasts, moving toward Kyiv and Sumy, suggesting preparation for targeted attacks (Source: @kpszsu/63514).
A Ukrainian strike hit an oil‑refining plant in Saratov, Russia, demonstrating offensive capability deep within Russian territory (Source: @UkraineNow/81583).
Ukraine received a new IRIS‑T surface‑to‑air missile system launcher, enhancing its air‑defence posture (Source: @UkraineNow/81583).
Ukrainian authorities reported multiple UAV sightings in Kirovohrad heading to Kropyvnytskyi, indicating cross‑regional reconnaissance or strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/63522).
Russian forces used 16 UAVs in the attack on the Starobelsk college, resulting in 70 injured, 21 dead, and extensive building damage (Source: @tass_agency/378497).
Three FAB‑250 aerial bombs were dropped on Druzhkivka, injuring five civilians and striking multi‑storey residential buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81596).
Russian authorities reported a rescue operation after the Starobelsk college strike lasting 45 hours with repeated threat of further attacks (Source: @tass_agency/378495).
A Russian‑linked oil tanker Tagor was intercepted by French forces in the Atlantic, highlighting ongoing sanctions enforcement (Source: @UkraineNow/81589).
Russian officials announced a meeting in the Kremlin to investigate the Starobelsk attack and named several Ukrainian commanders as suspects (Source: @tass_agency/378468).
UAV activity was reported in Sumy oblast, with drones heading toward Sumy, Konotop, and other towns, indicating ongoing reconnaissance or strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/63524).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike on a hospital building in Konotop, Sumy region, with no casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81596).
Reports from Chernihiv region mention UAVs moving along the border toward Kyiv, suggesting potential cross‑border incursions (Source: @kpszsu/63535).
In the Donetsk area, Russian aerial bombs were used in Druzhkivka, causing civilian injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81596).
Ukrainian intelligence noted UAV groups from Kursk region moving toward Romny, indicating cross‑border drone activity (Source: @kpszsu/63533).
Multiple UAVs were observed over Chernihiv region, including strike‑capable models moving from the north toward Kyiv (Source: @kpszsu/63514).
UAVs were reported in the north of Sumy heading toward Sumy city and surrounding areas (Source: @kpszsu/63507).
UAV groups from Kirovohrad were seen heading to Kropyvnytskyi, indicating operational reach across central Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/63522).
Chernihiv reconnaissance reported two reactive UAVs moving along the border near Slavutych at speeds over 500 km/h (Source: @eRadarrua/94666).
UAV activity in the north of Chernihiv included three reactive drones along the border toward Kyiv (Source: @eRadarrua/94634).
Shahed drones were launched against southern coastal cities, including one over Odessa and multiple over the Black Sea heading toward Odessa (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67319).
A massive Shahed launch targeted Primorsko‑Ahtarsk, Shatalove, Millerovo, Hvardiyiske, Orel, and Kursk with up to 194 drones in an hour (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67333).
Russian forces attacked a maternity ward in Odessa, damaging the facade and over 30 windows but causing no casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81599).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike on a Russian oil‑refining plant in Saratov, indicating deep‑strike capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81583).
Air‑raid alerts over Kyiv were active during the period, reflecting heightened threat levels across the southern‑northern axis (Source: @eRadarrua/94636).
Air‑raid alerts were issued over Kyiv at 20:14 and lifted at 20:39 on 01‑06‑2026, indicating active aerial threat (Source: @eRadarrua/94636, @eRadarrua/94660).
Russian forces deployed FAB‑250 aerial bombs on Druzhkivka, causing civilian injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81596).
Ukrainian forces used Shahed drones in large numbers against multiple targets, reflecting a significant UAV component in the air war (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67333).
UAVs equipped with Starlink antennas were identified on Ukrainian drones used in the Starobelsk college strike (Source: @tass_agency/378501).
Ukrainian air‑defence received an IRIS‑T missile system, enhancing capability against aerial threats (Source: @UkraineNow/81583).

key_developments: Ukrainian forces launched an estimated 97‑194 Shahed drones in an hour against six southern locations, indicating a coordinated large‑scale strike (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67333).
Russian attack on the Starobelsk college employed 16 UAVs, resulting in 70 injuries and 21 fatalities (Source: @tass_agency/378497).
Multiple strike‑capable UAVs were observed over Chernihiv and Sumy oblasts, moving toward Kyiv and Sumy, suggesting preparation for targeted attacks (Source: @kpszsu/63514).
Ukraine received a new IRIS‑T surface‑to‑air missile system launcher, strengthening its air‑defence network (Source: @UkraineNow/81583).
Ukrainian forces struck an oil‑refining plant in Saratov, Russia, demonstrating offensive reach deep into Russian territory (Source: @UkraineNow/81583).
Three FAB‑250 aerial bombs were dropped on Druzhkivka, injuring five civilians and hitting multi‑storey buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81596).
Air‑raid alerts were active over Kyiv at 20:14 and lifted at 20:39 on 01‑06‑2026, reflecting ongoing aerial threat (Source: @eRadarrua/94636).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian drone operations have escalated, with large Shahed launches targeting multiple southern settlements, indicating a shift toward massed UAV offensives. Russian UAV and aerial bomb attacks continue to inflict civilian casualties, notably the high‑profile strike on the Starobelsk college. The proliferation of strike‑capable UAVs across northern fronts suggests increased reconnaissance and potential for localized strikes, raising the overall threat level.
sections.regional_response: Western partners have supplied advanced air‑defence assets, such as the IRIS‑T system, to bolster Ukrainian defenses against the intensified UAV threat. Russia continues to leverage aerial bombardment, including FAB‑250 bombs, to pressure civilian infrastructure in contested areas. International sanctions actions, like the interception of the Tagor tanker, demonstrate ongoing pressure on Russian logistics.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths shows a pattern of north‑to‑south movements, with drones from Chernihiv heading toward Kyiv and from Kirovohrad toward central targets. The use of Starlink‑equipped drones in the Starobelsk attack highlights Ukraine's integration of commercial satellite communications for UAV control. Continued air‑raid alerts in Kyiv underscore the need for sustained civil defence readiness.
